since_last: 69% of 86 AI names green; AI Chips & Compute leads +7.04%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: Markets closed — Juneteenth
cards.body: US equity markets are shut for Juneteenth, so the tape is frozen at the previous session's close. Everything below reflects that last close — not live trading — and refreshes when the market reopens.

cards.headline: Last close: a broad bid, 69% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +1.08% · BTC +0.30% · Gold -1.21% · Nasdaq100 +2.48%. At the last close, 69% of 86 AI names were green — a broad bid. AI Chips & Compute did the heavy lifting at +7.04%.

cards.headline: AI Chips & Compute leads, AI Applications & Data Software lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is AI Chips & Compute at +7.04%; weakest is AI Applications & Data Software at -0.40%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: SMR is the standout at +12.86%
cards.body: Leaders: SMR +12.86%, INTC +10.80%, CEVA +10.71%, SMCI +10.58%, MU +10.43%. Laggards: INOD -7.19%, SPCX -5.28%, IBM -4.86%, WDAY -3.81%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -5.28% at $181.69
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -5.28% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: TER moving on no clear catalyst
cards.body: TER is +7.82% with no fresh headline this cycle — attention without a story. Volume, not news, is doing the work.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
